# Claude Code Automations

Optional `.claude/settings.json` automations and MCP servers that make working with TinaCMS smoother in Claude Code sessions.

## `.claude/settings.json` template

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit|Write",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "prompt",
            "prompt": "If the file path contains 'tina/__generated__/' or ends with 'tina-lock.json', BLOCK this edit. These files are auto-generated by tinacms build and must never be manually edited."
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit|Write",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "pnpm prettier --write $CLAUDE_FILE_PATH 2>/dev/null || true"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(pnpm tinacms dev:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm tinacms build:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm dlx @tinacms/cli@latest:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm dev:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm build:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm install:*)",
      "Bash(npm view tinacms*:*)",
      "Bash(npm view @tinacms/*:*)"
    ]
  }
}
```

## What each piece does

### PreToolUse hook — block edits to generated files

Claude Code agents sometimes try to "fix" type errors by editing `tina/__generated__/`. That's the wrong fix — the right fix is to update `tina/config.ts` and rerun `tinacms build`. This hook blocks the wrong fix:

- Matches `Edit` or `Write` tool calls
- If the file path contains `tina/__generated__/` or ends with `tina-lock.json`, blocks the edit
- The agent then has to look upstream at the schema

### PostToolUse hook — auto-format

Most projects use Prettier. Auto-format every edit/write so generated diffs stay clean.

### Permissions allowlist

Pre-approves the safe commands so the agent doesn't get permission-prompt fatigue. Add others as you discover them. The `:` after each command pattern is a wildcard for arguments.

## Recommended MCP servers

```bash
# Live TinaCMS / Next.js / React docs
claude mcp add context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest

# Browser-driving for testing visual editing
claude mcp add playwright -- npx -y @anthropic-ai/mcp-server-playwright@latest
```

`context7` is the highest-value MCP for TinaCMS work — TinaCMS' docs aren't in most LLM training data, and `context7` can fetch current docs on demand.

## Project-level skills (optional)

If you build TinaCMS sites repeatedly, consider creating project-level skills:

| Skill | What it does |
|---|---|
| `/new-block` | Scaffolds a block template + React component + renderer mapping |
| `/new-collection` | Adds a new content collection to `tina/config.ts` with sensible defaults |
| `/tina-audit` | Runs `tinacms audit` + checks for missing `ui.itemProps` and broken `tinaField` |
| `/tina-deploy` | Pre-deploy checklist (env vars, build script order, deploy hooks) |

These live at `.claude/skills/` in the project. Don't add them to your global skills — they're project-specific.

## Caveats

- **Hooks run on every Edit/Write.** The PostToolUse format hook can slow down rapid edits. If it's annoying, scope the matcher to `*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,md,mdx}` only.
- **Permissions are session-only by default.** They don't persist across sessions unless saved to `~/.claude/settings.json` (global) or `.claude/settings.json` (project).
- **MCP server installs persist.** They survive across sessions.

## When to skip these

If you're a one-off TinaCMS user (single project, won't touch it again), skip the automations. They're worth the setup cost for teams or for repeat-use.

## Verifying

```bash
# Verify hooks are loaded
cat .claude/settings.json | grep -A 2 PreToolUse

# Verify MCP servers
claude mcp list
```

Try an edit to `tina/__generated__/client.ts` — the hook should block it.
